meta.title: End Behavior of f(x) = −x¹⁰ − 3x⁹ + x² | MAT 170 Exam 2

steps.content: Determine the end behavior of:  f(x) = −x¹⁰ − 3x⁹ + x²
steps.narration: Determine the end behavior of f of x equals negative x to the tenth minus 3 x to the ninth plus x squared.
steps.label: MAT 170 Exam 2 Review

steps.narration: Here is our function: negative x to the tenth minus 3 x to the ninth plus x squared.

steps.content: Leading Coefficient Test: The end behavior of a polynomial is determined ONLY by its leading term (the term with the highest power of x).
steps.narration: The Leading Coefficient Test tells us end behavior depends only on the leading term.
steps.label: Leading Coefficient Test

steps.narration: The leading term is negative x to the tenth.
The degree is 10, which is even.
The leading coefficient is negative 1, which is negative.

steps.content:
Even degree + Negative leading coefficient:
• As x → −∞, f(x) → −∞  (falls to the left)
• As x → +∞, f(x) → −∞  (falls to the right)
Both ends point DOWN.
steps.narration: The pattern for even degree with a negative leading coefficient: both ends fall. The graph falls to the left and falls to the right. Think of it as an upside-down parabola shape on the ends.
steps.label: End Behavior Pattern

steps.narration: As x goes to negative infinity, f of x goes to negative infinity. The graph falls on the left.
As x goes to positive infinity, f of x also goes to negative infinity. The graph falls on the right too.

steps.content:
End behavior:  Falls left, Falls right  (both ends down)
As x → −∞, f(x) → −∞
As x → +∞, f(x) → −∞
steps.narration: The end behavior is: both ends fall. The graph goes down on the left and down on the right.
steps.label: Final Answer
